commits.author: Jérémy Franck
commits.subject: [hide] Remove repetition in test, simplify branching for SVG resolution

commits.author: Jérémy Franck
commits.subject: ETC-696: Trim numeric src, lock in fallback paths, fix stale render race
commits.body:
- Both renderers now trim src before the numeric check, so values like " 32 " from dynamic data resolve correctly
- PHP fallback is now explicit (clear src to '') rather than relying on SvgLoader's incidental handling of non-URL
  strings
- HtmlBlock $effect returns a stale flag cleanup to prevent an in-flight getActualSvg() from overwriting a newer result
- Tests added for: non-SVG attachment, missing ID, whitespace-padded ID

commits.author: Jérémy Franck
commits.subject: feat(etch/svg): Accept media library attachment ID for dynamic SVG
commits.body:
When src is a numeric string, resolve it to the attachment's URL if the
mime is image/svg+xml. Applied in both renderers — PHP SvgBlock for the
published page, Svelte SvgResolver for the builder preview. Non-SVG or
missing IDs fall back to the default SVG.

commits.author: Reinhard Sanz
commits.subject: chore: turn on PROPS_PANEL_REDESIGN and ENABLE_COMPONENT_NAMESPACE

commits.author: Reinhard Sanz
commits.subject: fix(ETC-694): Revert unwanted special behavoir regrding media id resolving

ETC-696.title: Add ID support for dynamic SVG
ETC-696.description:
## Acceptance Criteria

- [X] When an SVG `src` resolves to a WordPress media attachment ID for an SVG file, the SVG renders correctly in the Builder preview.
- [X] When an SVG `src` resolves to a WordPress media attachment ID for an SVG file, the SVG renders correctly on the frontend.
- [X] When the attachment ID does not exist or points to a non-SVG file, the default fallback SVG renders instead.

## Technical decisions:

* Numeric `src` values are treated as WordPress media attachment IDs.
* Non-SVG and missing attachments intentionally fall back to the default SVG.

ETC-695.title: Allow conditions within conditions
ETC-695.description:
[https://magnified-web.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/b997a63b-f0da-4774-945c-a65b7a2ac3d1](<https://magnified-web.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/b997a63b-f0da-4774-945c-a65b7a2ac3d1>)

AC: 
User can use a condition prop within a condition prop and it's value renders as expected.

ETC-694.title: Media Prop resolves to URL on canvas instead of keeping the ID
ETC-694.description:
[https://community.etchwp.com/c/announcements/1-4-14#comment_wrapper_101797161](<https://community.etchwp.com/c/announcements/1-4-14#comment_wrapper_101797161>)

### 🐞 Summary

A `wpMediaId` component prop is resolved to the attachment URL on the builder canvas, but stays as an ID on the frontend. Passing the ID into `{props.media}` shows the wrong value in the preview.

### 🔁 Reproduction

1. Open a page with a component that has a `Media prop` specialized as `wpMediaId`.
2. Render `Media prop: {props.media}` as text.
3. Observe:
   * **Canvas:** `Media prop: [URL to the media here]`
   * **Frontend:** `Media prop: [ID of the media here]`

### 🔍 Likely culprit

Commit `d1e3c9390` — *fix(*[ETC-635](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-635/redesign-media-prop-input)*): resolve wpMediaId props to URL when rendering on canvas* — added a `resolveWpMediaIdProperty` branch in `src/frontend/_utilities/ComponentPropertyResolver.ts` that unconditionally converts ID → URL for every `wpMediaId` prop on the canvas. The PHP resolver does not do this, hence the canvas/frontend divergence.
